code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a readable sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject to build coherent sentences
    airport_subjects = set()
    for t in triples:
        if "Airport" in t.subject or "airport" in t.subject.lower():
            airport_subjects.add(t.subject)

    # Find the main airport subject (if any)
    main_subject = list(airport_subjects)[0] if airport_subjects else triples[0].subject

    # Template mapping for all predicates
    templates = {
        "cityServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the city of {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda s, o: f"{s} is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": lambda s, o: f"{s} has runway {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway {o} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway {o} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} has runway number {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} runway surface is {o}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} first runway surface is {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} second runway surface is {o}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} third runway surface is {o}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} fourth runway surface is {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} fifth runway surface is {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a third runway {o} feet long",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fourth runway {o} feet long",
        "5thRunwayNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} has runway number {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has ICAO code {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has location code {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has IATA code {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is known locally as {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"{s} has capital {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"{s} official language is {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by {o}",
        "mayor": lambda s, o: f"{s} mayor is {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by the {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"{s} leader title is {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda s, o: f"{s} largest city is {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda s, o: f"{s} county seat is {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the {o} region",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"{s} currency is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"{s} residents are called {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda s, o: f"{s} hub airport is {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"{s} headquarters is at {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded by {o}",
        "foundingYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"{s} postal code is {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda s, o: f"{s} area code is {o}",
        "chief": lambda s, o: f"{s} chief is {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda s, o: f"{s} has jurisdiction over {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "class": lambda s, o: f"{s} class is {o}",
        "division": lambda s, o: f"{s} division is {o}",
        "order": lambda s, o: f"{s} order is {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates {o} helicopters",
        "transportAircraft": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates {o} transport aircraft",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates {o} fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates {o} attack aircraft",
        "battle": lambda s, o: f"{s} participated in the {o}",
    }

    # Build sentence parts
    sentence_parts = []
    city_country_info = {}

    # First pass: collect city and country relationships
    for t in triples:
        if t.predicate == "cityServed":
            city_country_info["city"] = t.object
        elif t.predicate == "country":
            city_country_info["country"] = t.object

    # Second pass: build sentence with main subject
    for i, t in enumerate(triples):
        if t.predicate == "cityServed":
            # Special handling for cityServed - it often starts the sentence
            if t.subject == main_subject:
                sentence_parts.append(f"{t.subject} serves the city of {t.object}")
            else:
                tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, lambda s, o: f"{s} {t.predicate} {o}")
                sentence_parts.append(tmpl(t.subject, t.object))
        elif t.predicate == "country":
            # Link country to city if available
            if "city" in city_country_info:
                sentence_parts.append(f"which is in {t.object}")
            else:
                sentence_parts.append(f"{t.subject} is in {t.object}")
        elif t.predicate == "capital" and "country" in city_country_info:
            sentence_parts.append(f"where the capital is {t.object}")
        else:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, lambda s, o: f"{s} {t.predicate} {o}")
            sentence_parts.append(tmpl(t.subject, t.object))

    # Join into coherent sentence
    if len(sentence_parts) == 1:
        return sentence_parts[0] + "."
    elif len(sentence_parts) == 2:
        return sentence_parts[0] + ", " + sentence_parts[1] + "."
    else:
        # Create complex sentence structure
        result = sentence_parts[0]
        for part in sentence_parts[1:-1]:
            result += ", " + part
        result += ", and " + sentence_parts[-1] + "."
        return result

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a readable sentence."""
      if not triples:
          return ""
      # Simple template mapping for common predicates
      templates = {
          "cityServed": "{subject} serves {object}",
          "location": "{subject} is located in {object}",
          "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{subject} lies {object} meters above sea level",
          "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{subject} lies {object} feet above sea level",
          "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "{subject} lies {object} metres above sea level",
          "operatingOrganisation": "{subject} is operated by {object}",
          "runwayLength": "{subject} has a runway length of {object} metres",
          "runwayName": "{subject} has runway {object}",
          "country": "{subject} is in {object}",
          "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {object}",
          "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "{subject} has a first runway {object} feet long",
          "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "{subject} has a first runway {object} metres long",
          "1stRunwayNumber": "{subject} has runway number {object}",
          "runwaySurfaceType": "{subject} runway surface is {object}",
          # generic fallback will be used for any other predicates
      }
      phrases = []
      for t in triples:
          tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
          if tmpl:
              phrase = tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=t.object)
          else:
              phrase = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}"
          phrases.append(phrase)
